title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - XVI
content:
It nods and curtseys and recovers
    When the wind blows above,
    The nettle on the graves of lovers
    That hanged themselves for love.

    The nettle nods, the wind blows over,
    The man, he does not move,
    The lover of the grave, the lover
    That hanged himself for love.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Edward Housman delivers a powerful performance in "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - XVI"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
